bullets.text: Combined ratio was 92.7 for Q2 and 91.8 year-to-date; bodily injury trends were down in both frequency and severity, though management cautioned about reserving given the lack of a compelling thesis for sustained negative trends.
Written premium grew 5% companywide for Q2, building from 4% to 6% month-over-month; Progressive expected leading few results versus industry peers on the combination of growth and profitability.
Agency channel showed renewed strength with new business growth and states returning to health; Direct operations posted very attractive numbers with strong consumer acceptance of product messaging.
Progressive repurchased 3.3 million shares and retired ~$223 million of hybrid debt at 95 cents on the dollar in early July; debt-to-total capital fell to ~23-24% post-transaction.
Prior-year reserve development reduced reserves by $33.6 million in Q2 and $72.7 million year-to-date; Personal Injury Protection showed welcome signs of moderation in both frequency and severity.
Wisconsin's mandatory insurance law effective June 1st drove a notable spike in new applications; Commercial Auto posted an 89.6 combined ratio with signs of selective return to growth emerging.
Latest product design released in Personal Lines incorporating best R&D results; new packaging of usage-based insurance initiative was in early release stages, designed to address consumer concerns.
